metadata.description: Kanban board for Subscription Management - View and Upgrade/Downgrade. Contains original epic task (as reference) and broken-down subtasks (1-2 day units). See ../KANBAN-BREAKDOWN-GUIDE.md for breakdown details.
metadata.note: This file contains the original epic task. Broken-down subtasks should be added based on KANBAN-BREAKDOWN-GUIDE.md

tasks.title: Subscription Management - View and Upgrade/Downgrade
tasks.description: Create subscription management interface allowing store owners to view current subscription, upgrade/downgrade tiers, and manage billing cycle.
tasks.userStory: As a store owner, I want to view and manage my subscription tier so that I can control my costs and upgrade/downgrade as needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a store owner views subscription page, when they access it, then they see current tier, billing cycle, next billing date, and usage limits
Given a store owner wants to upgrade, when they select a higher tier, then prorated billing is calculated and subscription is updated immediately
Given a store owner wants to downgrade, when they select a lower tier, then change is scheduled for end of current billing period
Given a subscription change occurs, when processed, then the store owner receives email confirmation with billing details
Given a store owner views subscription, when they are on a tier with limits, then they see current usage (e.g., locations used vs. allowed)
Given feature access is tier-based, when subscription tier changes, then feature access is updated accordingly
tasks.businessValue: Enables self-service subscription management, reducing support burden and improving customer control.
tasks.technicalNotes: Integrate with Stripe subscription management API. Handle prorated billing calculations. Update feature flags based on tier. Prevent downgrades that would exceed current usage.
successMetrics: Subscription upgrade rate: 20%+ of Starter tier upgrade within 90 days
Self-service subscription changes: 90%+ of changes done without support
businessRules: Upgrades take effect immediately with prorated billing
Downgrades take effect at end of current billing period
Cannot downgrade if current usage exceeds lower tier limits
Billing cycle can be monthly or annual (annual provides discount)
tasks.note: This is the original epic task. The subtasks should be broken down into 1-2 day units based on KANBAN-BREAKDOWN-GUIDE.md
